commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Our brother, Timothy, is set at liberty. From which we may conjecture that Timothy had been a prisoner, though it is not known where, nor on what occasion. (Witham)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Know that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you. I suppose that they were not at all unfavorably disposed towards Timothy: wherefore he also put him forward. For Hebrews 13:23 know ye, he says, that our brother Timothy is set at liberty, with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you. Set at liberty, he says; from whence. I suppose he had been cast into prison: or if not this, that he was sent away from Athens. For this also is mentioned in the Acts.
